origin: harvested from a real failed trajectory (run 2026-07-18, qwen3.5-27b, 44 steps, agent_done=true)
mismatch: screen shows three plausible-looking keybindings; the file is Python dict-repr that VSCode rejects with 14 errors, so none of the bindings are in effect
_note: Checkpoints are NOT duplicated here — the catching set is the base task's own process_checks/vscode/<base_task>/checkpoints.json. Duplicating them would let the two copies drift. `expected_conditions` below is the assertion: plant the tamper, run the base checkpoints, and every verdict must match.

expected_outcome_verifier._note: The outcome verifier reaches 3/8 here for the WRONG reason — its strict json.loads cannot read the file at all. It reports the identical 3/8 on a perfectly correct JSONC config (see process_checks/runner/synthetic_golden.py, jsonc variant). Our checkpoints distinguish the two; the outcome verifier does not.
